props_visible: Geometric rounded-triangle relief shapes on wall (backlit with warm LED strips)
Rope-hung pendant lights with natural fiber/straw shades
Wishbone-style chairs with natural woven seats
Traditional kilim rug on floor (partial, lower left)
Brand logo (golden arrow/diamond motif, digital overlay — two sizes)
notable_visual_elements: Geometric backlit wall feature = contemporary take on traditional Islamic geometric pattern heritage
Rounded triangle shapes may reference traditional Saudi architectural elements
Natural fiber/rope pendant lights = heritage craft in contemporary fixture form
Wishbone chairs (Scandinavian design) sit comfortably alongside heritage elements — intentional global-local blend
Kilim rug grounds the space in Najdi/Saudi craft tradition

hospitality_cues: Kilim rug on floor = traditional Saudi craft as interior element
Warm backlit geometry echoes the lantern-light warmth of traditional Najdi interiors
cultural_notes.free_notes: The design vocabulary here is sophisticated: geometric LED wall = contemporary echo of Islamic geometric art tradition; rope pendants = Bedouin material culture; wishbone chairs = international design language. @aseeb.najd's interior design is doing the same cultural blending work that their food does: Najdi authenticity elevated to premium contemporary experience. Every interior shot reinforces that this is not a museum but a living expression of Najdi culture.

pattern_matches.notes: Geometric wall feature as architectural hero — interior design as brand content
Empty restaurant interior tells the full story of the experience promise
